policies.name: Release Approval Policy
policies.scope: release tags and public release state
rules: release tags require explicit human approval
green verification alone does not authorize release tagging

policies.name: Main Branch Access Policy
rules: do not revert user-authored changes without explicit request
destructive git operations require explicit approval

policies.name: Benchmark Review Policy
rules: benchmark claims must cite concrete artifact evidence
npm test and npm run smoke must both pass before green is reported
human-low / ai-high review conflicts must be recorded as benchmark failures, not treated as acceptable disagreement
benchmark green requires at least one weak-artifact rejection case that proves the reviewer can avoid false positives
review packets used for benchmark claims must state the target audience or buyer perspective, not only artifact-internal correctness

policies.name: Orchestrator Runtime Discipline Policy
policies.scope: orchestration claims and parent-ai execution discipline
rules: an orchestrator may not claim organized execution if tasking, joins, decisions, or review state were handled only in conversational prose without runtime artifacts
when parent orchestration is claimed, the runtime record must include concrete task, role-result, role-join, decision, or goal artifacts sufficient to reconstruct the loop
missing runtime evidence is a benchmark failure, not an acceptable style variation
operator convenience is not a valid reason to skip runtime recording for material orchestration steps
green status must not be reported when the orchestrator bypassed the runtime for stateful coordination work

policies.name: Project Need Validation Policy
rules: project creation must not proceed directly from a raw need
a validated need and a project charter are required before council approval and project creation
need validation may reject, defer, request evidence, reframe the need, or require experiment evidence before project creation
no project should be created when the need validation record recommends do-not-create-project or hold-project
discovery outputs may inform need validation but do not replace the need validation gate

policies.name: Runtime-Backed Answer Discipline Policy
policies.scope: direction, review, self-review, and retrospective claims
rules: direction, review, self-review, and retrospective answers must be runtime-backed
current artifact readout alone is insufficient for direction, review, self-review, or retrospective claims
at least one runtime command execution must be recorded for each such answer surface
execution log ref and refreshed artifact ref must both be present or the answer is incomplete
operator-facing surfaces must show whether the latest answer was runtime-backed, when it ran, and which commands executed
